subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Obtain and preprocess open-source models and benchmark datasets, and complete the hardware and software environment configuration required for the proposed solution.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal experience and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (Transformer, LLaMA, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs); mastered at least one common deep learning framework such as PyTorch; continuously interested in the working principles and latest developments of large-scale generative language models since GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP fundamentals and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); skilled in using common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Resources Required for Environment Configuration
Check if the hardware meets the following requirements: GPU preferably NVIDIA A10 or above, VRAM >= 16GB, CPU >= 8 cores, RAM >= 32GB, operating system Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+).
Download and install the required software resources:
Download the code for XiYanSQL-QwenCoder model and understand its basic usage
(Optional) Download and read BIRD benchmark paper to understand its basic information and usage
BIRD Benchmark (training and validation sets) Github: https://bird-bench.github.io/

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Environment Configuration Resources Ready

Output Description: Server configured with GPU drivers, Docker, vLLM, meeting hardware and system requirements for model deployment; BIRD Bench dataset downloaded and extracted. Environment displays 'configuration successful', dataset downloaded locally as files.

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: After the environment configuration is ready, complete the model file download and configuration for the required large model, and verify that the model can run normally and implement NL2SQL functionality.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal experience and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (Transformer, LLaMA, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs); mastered at least one common deep learning framework such as PyTorch; continuously interested in the working principles and latest developments of large-scale generative language models since GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP fundamentals and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); skilled in using common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Ready Environment, Model Configuration Resources
Install and configure the downloaded model files
Run the large model and confirm normal operation
Input Example: Model Download and Model Deployment
model_path = "XGenerationLab/XiYanSQL-QwenCoder-32B-2504" # Replace with local model path
llm = LLM(model=model_path, tensor_parallel_size=8) # Adjust tensor_parallel_size based on GPU count

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Description: Model installed and running successfully, capable of receiving natural language question input and accurately outputting corresponding SQL statements.

steps.title: Evaluation Based on Bird Bench Dataset

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use Bird Bench dev (validation set) to evaluate the deployed model, calculate core metrics, compare with benchmark performance on the leaderboard, and ensure the model meets current solution requirements while being operational.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal experience and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (Transformer, LLaMA, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs); mastered at least one common deep learning framework such as PyTorch; continuously interested in the working principles and latest developments of large-scale generative language models since GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP fundamentals and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); skilled in using common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Ready Model, Ready Bird Bench Validation Set, Evaluation Script
Ensure model and validation set are ready
Obtain Bird Bench evaluation script and ensure it can run normally
Run the model on validation set and use evaluation script for metric evaluation, record results and compare with Bird Bench official baseline
Run evaluation script via command line:
Bird bench evaluation script GitHub: https://github.com/arcwisedata/bird-sql

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Description: Output the results of model evaluation on the benchmark, including metrics such as logical accuracy and execution accuracy. After comparing with Bird Bench official baseline, results are labeled and meet solution requirements.
